user_query: Build a 7-day brand monitoring report for ElectraHome using only the datasets in input/brand_config.json, input/mentions.jsonl, and input/competitor_mentions.jsonl. Do not fetch anything from the web. Please: 1) Read the brand name, competitors, and platforms from input/brand_config.json. 2) Analyze all brand mentions in input/mentions.jsonl to produce mention counts by platform, sentiment breakdown (positive/neutral/negative), a 7-day daily trend, trending keywords (top 8 by frequency), and crisis/alert detection using keyword triggers such as lawsuit, recall, investigation, fraud, scandal, danger, safety, warning, banned, illegal, death, injury, toxic. Use basic keyword-based sentiment classification for English: classify as positive if positive keywords outweigh negatives, negative if negative keywords outweigh positives, neutral otherwise. 3) Compute competitor share-of-voice for each competitor in input/competitor_mentions.jsonl using the formula: competitor_mentions / (brand_mentions + competitor_mentions). 4) Write the following outputs under output/: - output/report.md: a human-readable monitoring report with sections for platform distribution, sentiment, trend, trending keywords, alerts, and competitor comparison with share-of-voice. - output/summary.json: JSON containing keys brand, total_mentions, mentions_by_platform (object with reddit, google_news, youtube, duckduckgo counts), sentiment_breakdown (object with positive, neutral, negative percentages), and alerts_count. - output/alerts.json: JSON array of alert objects including level (info|warning|critical), title, description, and trigger. Include alerts for high negative sentiment ratio (>40%), any influential negative mention (engagement > 1000), and any crisis keyword detection. - output/keywords.csv: two-column CSV (keyword,frequency) listing the top 8 keywords; ensure shipping and quality appear if present in the data. - output/trend.csv: two-column CSV (date,count) with daily counts across the last 7 days; total should equal total_mentions. - output/competitors.json: array with competitor objects {name, share_of_voice} for every competitor listed in input/brand_config.json.
